explanation: The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV mode and the RM button is pressed (bool_exp2), then eventually (F) the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure will be active (bool_exp3). This captures that it shall be possible to initiate the RM procedure in PCV mode by pushing the button.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PCV mode and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) button is pressed, the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure shall be active. This captures the requirement that it must be possible to initiate the RM procedure by pressing the button while in PCV mode.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PCV mode and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) button is pressed, then eventually the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure will be active. This captures the requirement that it 'shall be possible to initiate' the RM procedure via the button in PCV mode.

The LTL property uses template 11, which asserts a global implication: G (bool_exp2 -> (X bool_exp3)). This means "Always, if bool_exp2 is true, then bool_exp3 will be true in the next state." In this context, bool_exp2 is set to "PCVMode & RMButton", representing the condition that the system is in PCV mode AND the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) button is pressed/set. Bool_exp3 is set to "RM", indicating that the Recruitment Maneuver procedure is active. Therefore, the property translates to: "Always, if the system is in PCV mode and the RM button is pressed, then the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure will be initiated (become active) in the very next state." This accurately captures the requirement that the RM procedure shall be possible to initiate with a button push while in PCV mode, implying a direct and immediate (next state) consequence.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PCV mode and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) button is pressed, the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure will eventually become active. This directly translates the requirement that in PCV mode, it shall be possible to initiate the RM procedure with the push of a single button.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV mode (bool_exp1) and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) button is pressed (bool_exp2), then in the very next state (X), the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure will be active (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that initiating the procedure via a button press in PCV mode should lead to the procedure's immediate activation.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV mode (PCVMode) and the Recruitment Maneuver button is pressed (RMButton), then the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) will eventually (F) be active. This captures that initiating the procedure is possible via the button press in the specified mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV mode (bool_exp1) AND the Recruitment Maneuver button is pressed (bool_exp2), then the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure will eventually become active (F bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that it shall be possible to initiate the RM procedure with the push of a single button while in PCV mode.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in PCV mode and the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) button is pressed, the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure will be initiated (become active) in the very next state. This accurately captures the requirement that it shall be possible to initiate the RM procedure with a button push when in PCV mode.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV mode AND the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) button is set (indicating a push or activation via GUI), then eventually (F) the Recruitment Maneuver (RM) procedure will be active. This captures the requirement that it 'shall be possible to initiate' the RM procedure when the conditions are met and the button is pushed, implying a guaranteed response.
